folio.label: 151v
folio.l: And thus he sit , til it was passed péme
Daun Ioún was risen , in the morwe also
And in the Gardyn , walketú to and froo
And hatú hise thynges seid , ful curteisly
¢ This goode wyf cam walkyngö , preuyly
In to the Gardyn , there he walketú softe
And hi~ saluetú , as she hatú don ofte
A maide child , cam in hire compaignye
Whicú as hire liste , she may gouäne and gye
For ©et vnder the ©erde , was the mayde
O deere Cosyn myn , Daun Ioún she seyde
What eiletú ©ow , so rathe for to rise
¢ Nece quod he , it ougúte I now suffise
Fyue houres for to slepe , vp on a nygút
But it were , for an old , appalled wygút
As ben theise wedded men , that lye and dare
As in a fourme , syt a wery hare
Were al forstraugút´ witú houndes grete and smale
But deere nece , why be ©e so pale
I trowe certes , that oure goode man
Hatú ©ow laboured , sitú the nygút bigan
That ©ow were nede , to resten hastily
And witú that word , he low ful merily
And of his owne thougút , he wex al reed
¢ This faire wyf , gan for to shake hire hed
And seide thus , ©a god wot al q¶ she
Nay Cosyn myn , it stant nat so witú me
For by that god , that ©af me soule and lyf
In al the Reame of Fraunce , is there no wyf
That lasse lust hatú , to that sory pley
For I may synge allas , and weylawey
That I was born~ , but to no wygút q¶ she
Dar I nat telle , how that it stant witú me
Wherfore I thynke , out of this lond to wende
Or elles , of my self , to make an ende
So ful am I of drede , and eke of care
¢ This Monk bygan , vp on this wyf to stare
And seide allas my Nece , god forbede
That ©e for any sorwe , or any drede
For do ©oure self , but telle me ©oure grief
îauenture , I may in ©oure myschief
Counseile or helpe , and therfore telletú me
Al ©oure anoye , for it shal ben secree
